---
title: "Codebook for the pooled RCVS data: repeated cross-sections (2018, 2021, 2024) and the 2021–2024 panel"
author:
- affiliation: Director IRL EUSPb
  name: Ruslan Kuchakov
- affiliation: Researcher IRL EUSPb
  name: Dmitrii Serebrennikov
- affiliation: Researcher IRL EUSPb
  name: Dmitry Bobrikov
- affiliation: Assistant Professor in the Department of Criminology at the University of South Florida
  name: Alex Knorre
- affiliation: Associate Professor of Empirical Legal Studies at European University at Saint Petersburg
  name: Dmitry Skougarevskiy
date: '`r Sys.Date()`'
output:
  html_document:
    encoding: UTF-8
---

![](img/ipp_logo_eng.png){#id .class width="274" height="110px"}
![](img/eusp_logo_eng.png){.class width="274" height="px"}

```{r, setup, echo=F, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
library(kableExtra,quietly = T)
library(dplyr, quietly = T)
options(knitr.kable.NA = ' ')

codebook_path <- "../../data/supplementary_data/codebook_all_waves_eng.csv" # Input the full path to the file on your device for proper knitting 
codebook <- read.csv2(file = codebook_path, stringsAsFactors = F, sep = ",", encoding = "UTF-8")

original_cb <- codebook[grep("^Q|ID|IV", codebook$variable_id),]
original_cb <- rbind(original_cb[grep("ID|IVDur|Q2001", original_cb$variable_id),], original_cb[-grep("ID|IVDur|Q2001", original_cb$variable_id),])

constructed_cb <- codebook[-grep("^Q|ID|IV", codebook$variable_id),]

rownames(original_cb) <- NULL
rownames(constructed_cb) <- NULL
```

## Generated variables

All generated variables are created from the original variables obtained directly from the survey, but there are several important differences that may be critical when conducting research on constructed variables. 

First, for analysis purposes, we almost everywhere coded non-response and difficulty answering as ‘No’, i.e. 0, to obtain binary variables.  

Second, in many cases additional grouping was done (as, for example, with employment, which was recovered from single questions related to age and nature of labour). 

Third, the survey design itself was arranged so that many questions were only asked under a specific set of conditions (e.g., the question about medical care was only asked of those who said physical harm).   

We therefore advise using these variables with caution, checking the distributions of responses against the original variables. If you find errors or have questions, please email us at d.bobrikov@eu.spb.ru.

```{r, constructed_variables, echo=FALSE}
names(constructed_cb) <- c("Variable ID",
                     "Variable label",
                     "Encoding")

kable(constructed_cb, align = "l") %>%
  kable_styling(font_size = 10, full_width = F) %>%
  column_spec(1, bold = T) %>%
  collapse_rows(columns = 1:3, valign = "top")
```

## Original variables

```{r, original_variables, echo=FALSE}

names(original_cb) <- c("Variable ID",
                     "Variable label",
                     "Encoding")

kable(original_cb, align = "l") %>%
  kable_styling(font_size = 10, full_width = F) %>%
  column_spec(1, bold = T) %>%
  collapse_rows(columns = 1:3, valign = "top")
```
